words.definition_en: to admit or accept that something is true or that a situation exists
words.definition_vi: thừa nhận, công nhận sự tồn tại hoặc tính xác thực của điều gì đó
words.example: He acknowledged that there was a problem.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đã thừa nhận rằng có một vấn đề xảy ra.

words.definition_en: to spend a long time worrying and upsetting yourself about something
words.definition_vi: trăn trở, đau khổ, suy nghĩ cực nhọc về một quyết định
words.example: I've been agonising over this decision for weeks.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã phải trăn trở về quyết định này suốt nhiều tuần qua.

words.definition_en: a feeling of having no interest in or enthusiasm about anything
words.definition_vi: sự thờ ơ, sự vô cảm, không quan tâm
words.example: There is a growing apathy among young people towards politics.
words.example_vi: Sự thờ ơ đối với chính trị đang ngày càng gia tăng trong giới trẻ.

words.definition_en: to stay away from someone or something, or prevent something from happening
words.definition_vi: tránh, né tránh điều gì đó hoặc ngăn nó xảy ra
words.example: Try to avoid the city centre during rush hour.
words.example_vi: Hãy cố gắng tránh khu vực trung tâm thành phố vào giờ cao điểm.

words.definition_en: the way that someone acts, especially towards other people
words.definition_vi: hành vi, cách đối nhân xử thế
words.example: The students were on their best behaviour during the visit.
words.example_vi: Thái độ cư xử của ông ấy đối với nhân viên của mình là không thể chấp nhận được.

words.definition_en: to laugh quietly, especially in a private or amused way
words.definition_vi: cười thầm, cười nhẹ một mình
words.example: He chuckled to himself as he read the funny letter.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy cười thầm khi đọc bức thư hài hước đó.

words.definition_en: to make someone feel less worried, unhappy, or upset
words.definition_vi: an ủi, dỗ dành; sự an ủi
words.example: We tried to comfort her after she lost her job.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đã cố gắng an ủi cô ấy sau khi cô ấy bị mất việc.

words.definition_en: v: to organize and perform a particular activity; n: the way someone behaves
words.definition_vi: v: thực hiện, tiến hành; n: hạnh kiểm, tư cách đạo đức
words.example: The senator's conduct was questioned by the committee.
words.example_vi: Hành vi của vị thượng nghị sĩ đã bị ủy ban chất vấn.

words.definition_en: something that happens as a result of a particular action or set of conditions
words.definition_vi: hậu quả, kết quả (thường là tiêu cực)
words.example: You must accept the consequences of your actions.
words.example_vi: Quyết định đó có thể dẫn tới những hậu quả nghiêm trọng.

words.definition_en: the state of being happy and satisfied
words.definition_vi: sự mãn nguyện, sự hài lòng
words.example: He found great contentment in his new life in the country.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy tìm thấy sự mãn nguyện lớn lao trong cuộc sống mới ở vùng nông thôn.

words.definition_en: annoyed or quite angry
words.definition_vi: bực mình, cáu gắt
words.example: My mother was very cross with me for being late.
words.example_vi: Mẹ tôi đã rất bực mình với tôi vì tội đi muộn.

words.definition_en: the quality of being serious, calm, and deserving respect
words.definition_vi: phẩm giá, lòng tự trọng, sự tôn nghiêm
words.example: She handled her resignation with great dignity.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy đã từ chức một cách rất đường hoàng và tự trọng.

words.definition_en: a strong feeling of dislike for something that looks, smells, or tastes unpleasant
words.definition_vi: sự ghê tởm, sự phẫn nộ; làm ghê tởm
words.example: The smell of the garbage filled me with disgust.
words.example_vi: Mùi rác rưởi khiến tôi cảm thấy vô cùng ghê tởm.

words.definition_en: disappointed because you have discovered that someone or something is not as good as you believed
words.definition_vi: vỡ mộng, chán nản vì nhận ra sự thật không như mong đợi
words.example: Many voters are disillusioned with the government's performance.
words.example_vi: Nhiều cử tri cảm thấy vỡ mộng với sự điều hành của chính phủ.

words.definition_en: annoyed or bored with something that you have experienced for too long
words.definition_vi: chán ngấy, hết chịu nổi
words.example: I'm fed up with this rainy weather.
words.example_vi: Tôi chán ngấy cái thời tiết mưa gió này rồi.

words.definition_en: to laugh repeatedly in a quiet but uncontrolled way, often because you are nervous or embarrassed
words.definition_vi: cười khúc khích
words.example: The girls were giggling in the back of the classroom.
words.example_vi: Mấy cô bé đang cười rúc rích ở cuối lớp học.

words.definition_en: to look quickly at someone or something
words.definition_vi: liếc nhìn, nhìn lướt qua
words.example: He glanced at his watch and realized he was late.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy liếc nhìn đồng hồ và nhận ra mình đã muộn.

words.definition_en: to see someone or something for a very short time and not clearly
words.definition_vi: nhìn thoáng qua, loáng thấy
words.example: I caught a glimpse of the celebrity as she left the hotel.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã thoáng thấy vị ngôi sao đó khi cô ấy rời khỏi khách sạn.

words.definition_en: to show that you are happy about your own success or someone else's failure in an unpleasant way
words.definition_vi: hả hê, tự mãn (thường dựa trên thất bại của người khác)
words.example: I'm not here to gloat over your misfortune.
words.example_vi: Tôi không ở đây để hả hê trước sự rủi ro của bạn.

words.definition_en: sad and quiet
words.definition_vi: ủ rũ, buồn rầu
words.example: You look a bit glum today. What's wrong?
words.example_vi: Trông bạn hôm nay có vẻ hơi ủ rũ. Có chuyện gì vậy?

words.definition_en: to make an expression of pain, strong dislike, etc. in which the face twists in an ugly way
words.definition_vi: nhăn mặt, làm mặt xấu vì đau đớn hoặc khó chịu
words.example: She grimaced at the bitter taste of the medicine.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy nhăn mặt vì vị đắng của thuốc.

words.definition_en: to smile widely
words.definition_vi: cười toe toét
words.example: He was grinning from ear to ear after winning the race.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy cười toe toét sau khi thắng cuộc đua.

words.definition_en: to deal with, have responsibility for, or be in charge of
words.definition_vi: xử lý, giải quyết, điều khiển
words.example: I don't know how she handles such a stressful job.
words.example_vi: Tôi không biết làm cách nào mà cô ấy có thể quán xuyến được một công việc áp lực đến vậy.

words.definition_en: easily annoyed by someone's mistakes or because you have to wait
words.definition_vi: thiếu kiên nhẫn, nôn nóng
words.example: Don't be so impatient! We'll be there in five minutes.
words.example_vi: Đừng có nóng nảy thế chứ! Chúng ta sẽ tới đó trong năm phút nữa thôi.

words.definition_en: lack of energy; lack of desire or ability to move or change
words.definition_vi: sự trì trệ, tính quán tính
words.example: The company's failure was due to bureaucratic inertia.
words.example_vi: Sự thất bại của công ty là do sự trì trệ của bộ máy quan liêu.

words.definition_en: the way that you behave towards other people, especially when you are being polite
words.definition_vi: cách cư xử, lễ tiết
words.example: It's bad manners to talk with your mouth full.
words.example_vi: Vừa ăn vừa nói là bất lịch sự.

words.definition_en: a movement or set of movements needing skill and care
words.definition_vi: thao tác, sự vận động khéo léo; diễn tập
words.example: Reversing round a corner is a difficult manoeuvre.
words.example_vi: Lùi xe quanh góc cua là một kỹ thuật khó thực hiện.

words.definition_en: to complain in an annoying way, especially in a low voice
words.definition_vi: rên rỉ, than vãn, phàn nàn
words.example: Stop moaning about the cold and put a sweater on.
words.example_vi: Thôi than vãn về cái lạnh đi và mặc áo len vào.

words.definition_en: to laugh at someone or something in an unkind way
words.definition_vi: chế nhạo, nhạo báng
words.example: It's not nice to mock other people's accents.
words.example_vi: Chế nhạo giọng nói của người khác là điều không tốt.

words.definition_en: to fail to take care of someone or something properly
words.definition_vi: thờ ơ, bỏ mặc, xao nhãng
words.example: The garden had been neglected for years.
words.example_vi: Khu vườn đã bị bỏ bê trong nhiều năm trời.

words.definition_en: to look quickly and secretly at something, especially through a small opening
words.definition_vi: nhìn trộm, hé nhìn
words.example: He peeped through the keyhole to see who was in the room.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy nhìn trộm qua lỗ khóa để xem ai đang ở trong phòng.

words.definition_en: to look carefully or with difficulty at something
words.definition_vi: nhìn chăm chú, nhìn kỹ (do khó thấy)
words.example: She peered through the mist, trying to find the path.
words.example_vi: Thanh thiếu niên thường cảm thấy bị áp lực bởi bạn bè cùng trang lứa.

words.definition_en: to stop something from happening or someone from doing something
words.definition_vi: ngăn chặn, phòng ngừa
words.example: The police tried to prevent the protestors from entering the building.
words.example_vi: Cảnh sát đã cố gắng ngăn cản những người biểu tình tiến vào tòa nhà.

words.definition_en: to feel or show great happiness about something
words.definition_vi: vui mừng, hân hoan
words.example: The whole country rejoiced at the news of the victory.
words.example_vi: Cả nước vui mừng khôn xiết trước tin tức thắng trận.

words.definition_en: to feel angry or upset about a situation or about something that someone has done, especially because you think it is unfair
words.definition_vi: phẫn nộ, bực tức, không bằng lòng
words.example: She resents the fact that she is paid less than her colleagues.
collocations: resent being told what to do
words.example_vi: Cô ấy bực bội vì thực tế là mình được trả lương thấp hơn các đồng nghiệp.

words.definition_en: determined not to change your mind or not to give up a course of action
words.definition_vi: kiên quyết, quả quyết
words.example: She remained resolute in her belief that the project would succeed.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy vẫn kiên định với niềm tin rằng dự án sẽ thành công.

words.definition_en: to smile in a way that shows you are pleased with yourself or know something that someone else does not know
words.definition_vi: cười nhếch mép, cười mỉa
words.example: What are you smirking at?
collocations: wiping the smirk off one's face
words.example_vi: Bạn đang cười khẩy cái gì thế?

words.definition_en: to say something quickly in an angry way
words.definition_vi: quát mắng, nói gắt gỏng
words.example: 'Don't just stand there!' he snapped.
words.example_vi: 'Đừng có đứng đần ra đấy!' anh ta gắt gỏng.

words.definition_en: a planned way of doing something in order to achieve what you want
words.definition_vi: chiến thuật, thủ đoạn
words.example: The team's defensive tactics worked perfectly.
words.example_vi: Chiến thuật phòng thủ của đội đã hoạt động một cách hoàn hảo.

words.definition_en: extreme fear
words.definition_vi: sự kinh hoàng, sự khiếp sợ
words.example: The sound of the explosion filled everyone with terror.
words.example_vi: Tiếng nổ vang lên khiến mọi người tràn ngập trong sự kinh hoàng.

words.definition_en: to give someone a drug or medical treatment
words.definition_vi: cung cấp, đưa thuốc hoặc điều trị y tế cho ai đó
words.example: The nurse administered the medication.
words.example_vi: Y tá đã cho bệnh nhân uống thuốc.

words.definition_en: to take someone into a hospital for treatment
words.definition_vi: cho nhập viện
words.example: He was admitted to hospital with chest pains.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đã được nhập viện vì bị đau ngực.

words.definition_en: extreme physical or mental pain
words.definition_vi: sự đau đớn tột cùng (thể xác hoặc tinh thần)
words.example: She lay on the floor in agony.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy nằm trên sàn nhà trong sự đau đớn tột cùng.

words.definition_en: a substance that stops the effects of a poison
words.definition_vi: thuốc giải độc
words.example: There is no known antidote to this snake's venom.
words.example_vi: Hiện chưa có loại thuốc giải độc nào cho nọc của loài rắn này.

words.definition_en: a senior doctor in a hospital who specializes in a particular area of medicine
words.definition_vi: bác sĩ tư vấn, bác sĩ chuyên khoa cấp cao
words.example: The consultant recommended surgery.
words.example_vi: Bác sĩ tư vấn đã đề nghị làm phẫu thuật.

words.definition_en: a statement about what disease or problem someone has
words.definition_vi: sự chẩn đoán
words.example: The doctors are still waiting for a final diagnosis.
words.example_vi: Các bác sĩ vẫn đang chờ đợi kết quả chẩn đoán cuối cùng.

words.definition_en: to protect someone from a disease by giving them a vaccine
words.definition_vi: tiêm chủng
words.example: All children should be inoculated against measles.
words.example_vi: Tất cả trẻ em nên được tiêm phòng bệnh sởi.

words.definition_en: a painful or uncomfortable feeling in a part of the body
words.definition_vi: sự kích ứng, sự ngứa ngáy
words.example: The lotion can cause skin irritation in some people.
words.example_vi: Loại kem dưỡng da này có thể gây kích ứng da ở một số người.

words.definition_en: unable to feel anything in a particular part of your body
words.definition_vi: tê, tê liệt (cảm giác)
words.example: My fingers were numb with cold.
words.example_vi: Các ngón tay của tôi đã bị tê cứng vì lạnh.

words.definition_en: a place where very old or ill people live and are looked after
words.definition_vi: viện dưỡng lão
words.example: She decided to move into a nursing home.
words.example_vi: Bà ấy đã quyết định chuyển vào sống trong một viện dưỡng lão.

words.definition_en: the loss of the ability to move your body or a part of it
words.definition_vi: sự tê liệt
words.example: The accident left him with permanent paralysis of the legs.
words.example_vi: Vụ tai nạn đã khiến anh ấy bị liệt chân vĩnh viễn.

words.definition_en: a white substance that is used for protecting a broken bone; a small piece of sticky cloth to cover a cut
words.definition_vi: băng dán (cá nhân); thạch cao (bó bột)
words.example: His leg was in plaster for six weeks.
words.example_vi: Chân của anh ấy đã phải bó bột trong sáu tuần.

words.definition_en: to tell someone what medicine they should take
words.definition_vi: kê đơn thuốc
words.example: The doctor prescribed some antibiotics for my throat infection.
words.example_vi: Bác sĩ đã kê một số loại thuốc kháng sinh cho tình trạng viêm họng của tôi.

words.definition_en: medical treatment or advice that is intended to stop you from becoming ill
words.definition_vi: y học dự phòng
words.example: Preventive medicine is more cost-effective than curing diseases.
words.example_vi: Y học dự phòng mang lại hiệu quả kinh tế cao hơn so với việc chữa bệnh.

words.definition_en: a doctor's opinion about the likely progress of a disease or illness
words.definition_vi: tiên lượng (bệnh)
words.example: The prognosis for her recovery is very good.
words.example_vi: Tiên lượng về sự hồi phục của cô ấy là rất tốt.

words.definition_en: time away from work because you are ill
words.definition_vi: nghỉ ốm
words.example: He's been on sick leave for three weeks.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đã nghỉ phép vì bệnh được ba tuần rồi.

words.definition_en: an unwanted effect that a drug has on your body in addition to curing illness
words.definition_vi: tác dụng phụ
words.example: Nausea is a common side effect of this drug.
words.example_vi: Buồn nôn là một tác dụng phụ thường gặp của loại thuốc này.

words.definition_en: a medical instrument used for injecting or removing liquids
words.definition_vi: ống tiêm
words.example: The doctor filled the syringe with the vaccine.
words.example_vi: Bác sĩ đã nạp vắc-xin vào ống tiêm.

words.definition_en: a substance put into the body to prevent a specific disease
words.definition_vi: vắc xin
words.example: Scientists are developing a new vaccine for the virus.
words.example_vi: Các nhà khoa học đang phát triển một loại vắc-xin mới cho loại vi-rút này.

words.definition_en: a large room in a hospital where patients with similar conditions stay
words.definition_vi: khoa, phòng bệnh (trong bệnh viện)
words.example: He's on the children's ward.
words.example_vi: Cậu bé đang ở khoa nhi.

words.definition_en: suddenly become unconscious; make a place dark by turning off all the lights
words.definition_vi: bất tỉnh; cắt điện, làm tối om
words.example: I blacked out for a few seconds after the hit.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã bị ngất đi trong vài giây sau cú va chạm.

words.definition_en: become covered in spots or a rash
words.definition_vi: nổi ban, nổi mụn trên da
words.example: I've come out in a rash after eating those strawberries.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã bị nổi phát ban sau khi ăn những quả dâu tây đó.

words.definition_en: become conscious again; happen again; visit someone; change your opinion
words.definition_vi: tỉnh lại; lặp lại; ghé thăm; thay đổi ý kiến
words.example: She came round about an hour after the operation.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy đã tỉnh lại khoảng một giờ sau ca phẫu thuật.

words.definition_en: begin to realise or understand something
words.definition_vi: bắt đầu hiểu ra, nhận thức được
words.example: It took me a while to cotton on to what he meant.
words.example_vi: Tôi phải mất một lúc mới hiểu ra được ý anh ấy muốn nói là gì.

words.definition_en: laugh a lot, or make someone laugh a lot
words.definition_vi: cười nắc nẻ, làm ai đó cười ngất
words.example: The whole audience creased up at his jokes.
words.example_vi: Toàn bộ khán giả đã cười nghiêng ngả trước những câu đùa của anh ấy.

words.definition_en: check the health of someone; try to find out more about something
words.definition_vi: theo dõi (sức khỏe); tìm hiểu thêm, bám sát
words.example: The doctor will follow up with another appointment next month.
words.example_vi: Bác sĩ sẽ theo dõi tình hình bằng một cuộc hẹn khác vào tháng sau.

words.definition_en: make someone feel sad or lose hope
words.definition_vi: làm ai đó buồn chán, thất vọng
words.example: This rainy weather is really getting me down.
words.example_vi: Cái thời tiết mưa gió này thực sự làm tôi nản lòng.

words.definition_en: start to feel happy or well again after something bad; solve or deal with a problem
words.definition_vi: hồi phục, vượt qua (khó khăn, bệnh tật)
words.example: It took him a long time to get over the flu.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đã phải mất một thời gian dài mới khỏi được bệnh cúm.

words.definition_en: produce a particular reaction (well/badly) with someone
words.definition_vi: được đón nhận (tốt/xấu) bởi ai đó
words.example: The news didn't go down very well with the employees.
words.example_vi: Tin tức đó đã không được các nhân viên đón nhận một cách mấy tốt đẹp.

words.definition_en: try to hit or attack someone suddenly; speak angrily to or against someone
words.definition_vi: tấn công bất ngờ; quát tháo, xỉ vả
words.example: He lashed out at his critics in a speech yesterday.
words.example_vi: Ông ấy đã chỉ trích gay gắt những người phê bình mình trong bài phát biểu ngày hôm qua.

words.definition_en: die (a polite way to say 'die')
words.definition_vi: qua đời, mất (nói giảm nói tránh)
words.example: My grandfather passed away peacefully in his sleep.
words.example_vi: Ông nội tôi đã qua đời thanh thản trong giấc ngủ.

words.definition_en: cause difficulties or pain for someone; behave badly
words.definition_vi: gây rắc rối, làm đau; giở quẻ, cư xử tệ
words.example: My back is playing up again.
words.example_vi: Cái lưng của tôi lại bắt đầu giở quẻ rồi.

words.definition_en: manage to stay alive after being very ill; succeed in a difficult situation
words.definition_vi: hồi phục sau cơn bạo bệnh; vượt qua khó khăn
words.example: The doctors think he will pull through.
words.example_vi: Các bác sĩ nghĩ rằng anh ấy sẽ vượt qua được cơn nguy kịch.

words.definition_en: become smaller and thinner; become weaker
words.definition_vi: héo hon, teo lại, khô héo
words.example: The leaves shrivelled up in the heat.
words.example_vi: Lá cây đã bị héo rũ đi vì cái nóng.

words.definition_en: manage to produce a quality or reaction that helps you deal with a situation
words.definition_vi: thu hết (can đảm, sức lực), huy động
words.example: I finally summoned up the courage to ask her out.
words.example_vi: Cuối cùng tôi cũng đã lấy hết can đảm để mời cô ấy đi chơi.

words.definition_en: do something to prevent someone or something from harming you
words.definition_vi: ngăn chặn, đẩy lùi (nguy hiểm, bệnh tật)
words.example: She take vitamin C to ward off colds.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy uống vitamin C để phòng ngừa cảm lạnh.

words.definition_en: to do something for a particular purpose
words.definition_vi: hành động, đóng vai
words.example: He was acting on his lawyer's advice.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đã hành động theo lời khuyên của luật sư.

words.definition_en: manner of acting
words.definition_vi: hành vi
words.example: His behaviour towards his staff is unacceptable.
words.example_vi: Thái độ cư xử của ông ấy đối với nhân viên của mình là không thể chấp nhận được.

words.definition_en: result of an action
words.definition_vi: hậu quả
words.example: The decision could have serious consequences.
words.example_vi: Quyết định đó có thể dẫn tới những hậu quả nghiêm trọng.

words.definition_en: to produce tears
words.definition_vi: khóc
words.example: The baby started to cry for help.
words.example_vi: Đứa bé bắt đầu khóc để cầu cứu.

words.definition_en: no longer alive; completely
words.definition_vi: chết; hoàn toàn
words.example: There was dead silence in the room.
words.example_vi: Đã có một sự im lặng chết chóc trong căn phòng.

words.definition_en: a change that is caused by something
words.definition_vi: ảnh hưởng, tác động
words.example: The new law comes into effect next month.
words.example_vi: Đạo luật mới sẽ có hiệu lực vào tháng tới.

words.definition_en: to experience an emotion or physical sensation
words.definition_vi: cảm thấy
words.example: I feel strongly about this issue.
words.example_vi: Tôi có cảm nhận rất mạnh mẽ về vấn đề này.

words.definition_en: the state of being well
words.definition_vi: sức khỏe
words.example: Smoking is bad for your health.
words.example_vi: Hút thuốc lá có hại cho sức khỏe của bạn.

words.definition_en: not feeling well; sick
words.definition_vi: ốm, bệnh
words.example: He was taken ill during the meeting.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đã bị đổ bệnh ngay trong cuộc họp.

words.definition_en: caring about others; a group of things with the same features
words.definition_vi: tử tế; loại
words.example: It was very kind of you to help.
words.example_vi: Bạn thật tốt bụng khi đã giúp đỡ.

words.definition_en: to make sounds showing you are happy or think something is funny
words.definition_vi: cười
words.example: We had a good laugh about it later.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đã có một trận cười sảng khoái về chuyện đó sau này.

words.definition_en: the period between birth and death
words.definition_vi: cuộc sống, sinh mạng
words.example: The doctor saved his life.
words.example_vi: Bác sĩ đã cứu mạng anh ấy.

words.definition_en: to be alive; to have your home in a place
words.definition_vi: sống
words.example: You have to live within your means.
words.example_vi: Bạn phải sống trong khả năng kinh tế của mình.

words.definition_en: a substance used to treat an illness
words.definition_vi: thuốc; y học
words.example: Don't forget to take your medicine.
words.example_vi: Đừng quên uống thuốc đấy nhé.

words.definition_en: behaving in a way that is socially correct and shows respect
words.definition_vi: lịch sự
words.example: It's polite to say thank you.
words.example_vi: Nói lời cảm ơn là một hành động lịch sự.

words.definition_en: to behave in a particular way because of something that has happened
words.definition_vi: phản ứng
words.example: How did he react to the news?
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đã phản ứng như thế nào trước tin tức đó?

words.definition_en: the way someone acts or feels in response to something
words.definition_vi: sự phản ứng
words.example: What was his immediate reaction?
words.example_vi: Phản ứng tức thì của anh ấy là gì?

words.definition_en: an answer or reaction
words.definition_vi: sự phản hồi, câu trả lời
words.example: I'm still waiting for a response to my email.
words.example_vi: Tôi vẫn đang chờ phản hồi cho email của mình.

words.definition_en: physically or mentally ill; wanting to vomit
words.definition_vi: ốm; buồn nôn; chán ngấy
words.example: I'm sick and tired of your complaining.
words.example_vi: Tôi phát ốm và mệt mỏi với sự phàn nàn của bạn rồi.

words.definition_en: to behave towards someone in a particular way; to give medical care
words.definition_vi: đối xử; điều trị; chiêu đãi
words.example: They treat their animals cruelly.
words.example_vi: Họ đối xử với các loài vật của mình một cách tàn nhẫn.

words.definition_en: waste time feeling upset about something bad that has happened and cannot be changed
words.definition_vi: tốn thời gian buồn bã vì những chuyện đã rồi, không thể cứu vãn
words.example: It's a shame we lost the match, but there's no point crying over spilt milk.
words.example_vi: Thật đáng tiếc khi chúng ta để thua trận đấu, nhưng chẳng ích gì khi cứ hối tiếc về những chuyện đã rồi.

words.definition_en: suddenly become extremely angry without a good reason
words.definition_vi: mất bình tĩnh, đùng đùng nổi giận không rõ lý do
words.example: He tends to fly off the handle when things don't go his way.
words.example_vi: Anh ta có xu hướng nổi trận lôi đình khi mọi việc không theo ý mình.

words.word: give sb a taste/dose of their own medicine
words.definition_en: treat someone in the same bad way they treat other people
words.definition_vi: gậy ông đập lưng ông, đối xử tệ với ai đó y như cách họ đã làm
words.example: He's always late, so I'm going to give him a taste of his own medicine today.
words.example_vi: Anh ta lúc nào cũng đến muộn, thế nên hôm nay tôi sẽ cho anh ta nếm mùi gậy ông đập lưng ông.

words.definition_en: decide not to do something that you usually do
words.definition_vi: bỏ qua, không làm việc gì đó thường làm
words.example: I think I'll give the gym a miss tonight; I'm too tired.
words.example_vi: Tôi nghĩ đêm nay tôi sẽ bỏ qua buổi tập gym; tôi mệt quá rồi.

words.definition_en: accept a difficult situation without complaining or showing how you feel
words.definition_vi: cắn răng chịu đựng, chấp nhận hoàn cảnh khó khăn mà không phàn nàn
words.example: We just have to grin and bear it until the repairs are finished.
words.example_vi: Chúng ta đành phải cắn răng chịu đựng cho tới khi việc sửa chữa hoàn tất thôi.

words.definition_en: remain serious and not laugh
words.definition_vi: giữ vẻ mặt nghiêm túc, không cười
words.example: It was hard to keep a straight face when he started singing.
words.example_vi: Thật khó để giữ được vẻ mặt nghiêm túc khi anh ấy bắt đầu hát.

words.definition_en: used for telling someone not to get angry or upset
words.definition_vi: khuyên ai đó hãy bình tĩnh, đừng nổi nóng
words.example: Keep your hair on! I'm almost ready.
words.example_vi: Đừng nóng! Tôi sắp xong rồi đây.

words.definition_en: be very annoyed because you have made a mistake, missed an opportunity, etc.
words.definition_vi: tự dằn vặt mình, hối tiếc vì sai lầm
words.example: I'm kicking myself for not buying that house when I had the chance.
words.example_vi: Tôi đang tự dằn vặt mình vì đã không mua căn nhà đó khi có cơ hội.

words.definition_en: shout or do something that allows you to get rid of anger
words.definition_vi: xả hơi, giải tỏa cơn giận
words.example: I went for a run to let off some steam.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã đi chạy bộ để xả bớt căng thẳng.

words.definition_en: leave a person or situation alone if they might cause you trouble
words.definition_vi: chuyện cũ hãy để nó qua đi, đừng bới móc rắc rối
words.example: I thought about bringing up the argument again, but decided to let sleeping dogs lie.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã định nhắc lại cuộc tranh cãi đó, nhưng rồi quyết định để cho mọi chuyện được yên xuôi.

words.word: never/don't look a gift horse in the mouth
words.definition_en: if you are given something good, you should not complain about it or try to find things that are wrong with it
words.definition_vi: đừng đòi hỏi ở những món quà được tặng, được tặng thì đừng có chê
words.example: The car is old, but it was free. Don't look a gift horse in the mouth.
words.example_vi: Chiếc xe tuy cũ nhưng nó miễn phí mà. Đừng có kén cá chọn canh với đồ được tặng.

words.definition_en: angry and complaining about something
words.definition_vi: phẫn nộ, biểu tình phản đối kịch liệt
words.example: The local residents are up in arms about the new road project.
words.example_vi: Cư dân địa phương đang phản đối kịch liệt về dự án làm đường mới.

exercises.label: A
exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in the boxes in the correct form.

items.sentence: It's not very good ___ to eat with your mouth open, Timothy.
items.explanation: 'Manners' refers to social etiquette, like table manners.

items.sentence: I expect very good ___ from all of you while I'm out of the classroom.
items.explanation: 'Behaviour' refers to the way people act or conduct themselves in a specific situation.

items.sentence: There's no way you can ___ me from seeing my friends!
items.explanation: 'Prevent' means to stop someone from doing something.

items.sentence: Let's ___ the city centre as there'll be loads of traffic.
items.explanation: 'Avoid' means to stay away from a place.

items.sentence: The girls were ___ excitedly in their room - it was extremely irritating!
items.explanation: 'Giggle' describes high-pitched, repeated laughing often associated with children or nervousness.

items.sentence: He occasionally ___ quietly to himself as he read the letter.
items.explanation: 'Chuckle' is a quiet, low-toned laugh of amusement.

items.sentence: The boys were ___ after the film so I think they had a good time.
items.explanation: 'Grin' is a wide, happy smile.

items.sentence: Stop ___ in such an unpleasant way. Just because you won the game doesn't make you better than me, you know!
items.explanation: 'Smirk' is an unpleasant smile showing self-satisfaction or mocking.

items.sentence: I ___ something through the window but I've got no idea what it was.
items.explanation: 'Glimpse' means to see something for a very short time and not clearly.

items.sentence: Terry ___ at the clock and realised he was late.
items.explanation: 'Glance' is to look quickly at something intentionally.

items.sentence: The window was so dirty we had to ___ through it to see inside.
items.explanation: 'Peer' means to look closely or with difficulty.

items.sentence: Don't ___! Keep your eyes closed or it'll ruin the surprise.
items.explanation: 'Peep' means to look quickly and secretly.

items.sentence: I'm ___. It's boring just sitting here. Let's go out for a walk.
items.explanation: 'Fed up' means bored or annoyed with a situation that has lasted too long.

items.sentence: Mr Jenkins was so ___ when he heard the news he went red with anger.
items.explanation: 'Cross' is a word for being angry or annoyed.

items.sentence: Reversing round a corner is a difficult ___.
items.explanation: 'Manoeuvre' refers to a skilled movement, especially with a vehicle.

items.sentence: I'm going to have to use some underhand ___ to get Adam to agree.
items.explanation: 'Tactics' refers to planned methods (sometimes dishonest) used to achieve an aim.

exercises.label: B
exercises.instruction: Each of the words in bold, except one, is in the wrong place. Write the correct words on the lines.

items.sentence: In the playground, they often (1) ___ and ridicule the 'weaker' children...
items.explanation: 'Mock' fits because it means to ridicule or make fun of.

items.sentence: ...they (2) ___ openly.
items.explanation: 'Grin' (smile widely) fits the reaction to success.

items.sentence: When they're taking horrible medicine, they (3) ___ in a way that adults rarely do.
items.explanation: 'Grimace' (twist face in dislike/pain) fits taking horrible medicine.

items.sentence: When they're (4) ___, you know about it immediately.
items.explanation: 'Impatient' describes the state of children wanting something now.

items.sentence: The (5) ___ a young child can experience during a nightmare...
items.explanation: 'Terror' (extreme fear) fits the context of nightmares.

items.sentence: When something angers them, they (6) ___ instantly.
items.explanation: 'Snap' (speak angrily) fits a quick reaction to anger.

items.sentence: A young child can go from peaceful (7) ___ to extreme anger...
items.explanation: 'Contentment' (happiness/satisfaction) is the opposite of anger.

items.sentence: ...but then they might completely (8) ___ it.
items.explanation: 'Neglect' fits the idea of failing to care for a pet.

items.sentence: Firstly, humans have to learn how to (9) ___ and control their emotions.
items.explanation: 'Conduct' (behave/manage) oneself is the goal here.

items.sentence: Secondly, humans have to learn how to (10) ___ themselves within a society.
items.explanation: 'Handle' (deal with/manage) themselves fits the social context.

exercises.label: C
exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in the boxes in the correct form.

items.sentence: I've been ___ over this decision for ages but I've definitely made my mind up now.
items.explanation: 'Agonise over' means to worry intensely about a decision.

items.sentence: The whole country was out on the streets ___ when they won the World Cup.
items.explanation: 'Rejoice' means to show great happiness.

items.sentence: Do you at least ___ that not everyone thinks about things the same way you do?
items.explanation: 'Acknowledge' means to admit that something is true.

items.sentence: I was in such pain I just lay in bed ___.
items.explanation: 'Moan' refers to making low sounds of pain.

items.sentence: I'm ___ with the way this company's dealt with this problem. I'm going to complain.
items.explanation: 'Disgusted' shows strong disapproval/dislike.

items.sentence: I do ___ the fact that Josh was promoted and I wasn't, yes.
items.explanation: 'Resent' means to feel angry because of perceived unfairness.

items.sentence: We all need to ___ Joyce at such a difficult time for her.
items.explanation: 'Comfort' means to soothe or reassure someone.

items.sentence: Monica's absolutely determined to get this law changed. I've never seen her so ___ about anything before.
items.explanation: 'Resolute' means determined.

items.sentence: You look a bit ___ today. Is anything the matter?
items.explanation: 'Glum' means sad and quiet.

items.sentence: Joe said he's so ___ with his job he's thinking of quitting.
items.explanation: 'Disillusioned' means disappointed by something believed to be better.

items.sentence: No one knows exactly what the ___ of this action will be.
items.explanation: 'Consequences' are the results of an action.

items.sentence: I thought the Prime Minister handled her resignation speech with great ___.
items.explanation: 'Dignity' is a calm, serious manner that commands respect.

items.sentence: The bureaucratic system in this country is suffering from extreme ___. Nothing ever gets done!
items.explanation: 'Inertia' in this context means a lack of movement or change.

items.sentence: There's just so much ___ from young people these days when it comes to politics. They're just not interested.
items.explanation: 'Apathy' is a complete lack of interest.

exercises.label: D
exercises.instruction: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: If he's been bitten by a snake, we'll have to get him to a hospital to get the ___ immediately.
items.explanation: An antidote stops the effects of poison (venom).

items.sentence: They give most children a number of ___ these days to stop them getting serious diseases.
items.explanation: Vaccines prevent diseases.

items.sentence: Have you been ___ against tetanus?
items.explanation: Inoculate is specifically for giving a vaccine to protect against disease.

items.sentence: The symptoms are small red spots and slight skin ___.
items.explanation: 'Irritation' is the medical term for skin discomfort.

items.sentence: I've cut my finger. Have you got ___?
items.explanation: 'A plaster' refers to the individual sticky strip.

items.sentence: Richie's broken his leg and it's in ___.
items.explanation: 'In plaster' (uncountable) refers to the thạch cao (cast) for broken bones.

items.sentence: My ___ is that you're suffering from stress.
items.explanation: Diagnosis is identifying the current illness.

items.sentence: If you carry on taking the tablets, then your ___ is excellent.
items.explanation: Prognosis is the forecast of future recovery.

items.sentence: ___ medicine's all about not getting diseases rather than curing them after you've got them, isn't it?
items.explanation: The term is 'preventive medicine'.

items.sentence: My grandmother spent the last two years of her life in a nursing ___.
items.explanation: The term is 'nursing home'.

exercises.label: E
exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in the box in the correct form.

items.sentence: I woke up in (1) ___.
items.explanation: 'In agony' means in extreme pain.

items.sentence: I immediately arranged to go on sick (2) ___ from work.
items.explanation: 'Sick leave' is time off work when ill.

items.sentence: Then I called the doctor, who came that morning and (3) ___ some medicine for me.
items.explanation: Doctors 'prescribe' medicine.

items.sentence: He said I'd have to be (4) ___ to hospital.
items.explanation: Patients are 'admitted' to hospital.

items.sentence: ...and put me in a (5) ___ with about 20 other people.
items.explanation: A 'ward' is a room for patients in a hospital.

items.sentence: In the afternoon, the (6) ___ came to see me.
items.explanation: A senior specialist doctor.

items.sentence: Her (7) ___ was that I'd got some kind of ulcer...
items.explanation: Identification of the problem.

items.sentence: ...she said they'd have to (8) ___ antibiotics via an intravenous drip.
items.explanation: To give a drug to a patient.

items.sentence: ...I had a big (9) ___ sticking into my arm...
items.explanation: Instrument for injection.

items.sentence: One problem, though, was that the drugs did produce one strange (10) ___ effect.
items.explanation: 'Side effect' is an unwanted reaction to medicine.

items.sentence: The whole of the left side of my face went (11) ___.
items.explanation: Losing the ability to feel.

items.sentence: It wasn't exactly (12) ___ because I could still move my cheek...
items.explanation: Paralysis is the inability to move.

items.sentence: I don't know how I'm going to summon ___ the courage to tell Dad.
items.explanation: 'Summon up' means to gather courage/strength.

items.sentence: It really got me ___ when the doctor said I'd have to be off work for eight weeks.
items.explanation: 'Get someone down' means to make them feel sad.

items.sentence: Grandpa says his leg's playing ___ again so he's not going to come for a walk with us.
items.explanation: 'Play up' means to cause pain or function poorly.

items.sentence: Did it take June a long time to get ___ her glandular fever?
items.explanation: 'Get over' an illness means to recover from it.

items.sentence: We all creased ___ when Nick walked in wearing a skirt.
items.explanation: 'Crease up' means to laugh a lot.

items.sentence: Antony says he felt a little dizzy when he came ___ from the anaesthetic.
items.explanation: 'Come round' means to regain consciousness.

items.sentence: These carrots have shrivelled ___ a bit so I think I'll throw them out.
items.explanation: 'Shrivel up' means to become dry and wrinkled.

items.sentence: Jerry's come ___ in spots all over his face.
items.explanation: 'Come out in' means to have spots/rash appear on the skin.

exercises.label: G
exercises.instruction: Write a phrasal verb from the box in the correct form in each gap.

items.sentence: Mr Deacon next door had a very serious operation. Apparently, it's a miracle he ___.
items.explanation: 'Pull through' means to survive a serious illness/operation.

items.sentence: The first comedian didn't ___ well with the audience at all and he was booed off the stage.
items.explanation: 'Go down well/badly' refers to how something is received.

items.sentence: It took Terry a while to ___ to the fact I was joking.
items.explanation: 'Cotton on' means to begin to understand.

items.sentence: There's no point ___ at Dr Barker. She's not responsible for what's happened.
items.explanation: 'Lash out' means to attack with words or hits.

items.sentence: I don't know what happened. I was just sitting at my desk and I suddenly ___ for a few minutes.
items.explanation: 'Black out' means to lose consciousness.

items.sentence: In some cultures, they wear jewellery to ___ evil spirits.
items.explanation: 'Ward off' means to prevent something harmful.

items.sentence: The researchers ___ a number of patients who had taken the drug to determine what side effects they had experienced.
items.explanation: 'Follow up' means to check further/later.

items.sentence: Susie, I'm afraid I've got some bad news. Your grandmother ___ peacefully during the night.
items.explanation: 'Pass away' is a polite term for 'die'.

exercises.label: H
exercises.instruction: Tick the phrasal verbs in these sentences which contain the idea of completing something successfully.

items.sentence: It's going to be tough but we'll muddle through somehow, you'll see.
items.explanation: 'Muddle through' means to succeed despite difficulties/lack of plan.

items.sentence: I was going through my wardrobe when I found these jeans.
items.explanation: 'Going through' here means searching or examining.

items.sentence: I'm determined to see this through to the bitter end.
items.explanation: 'See through' means to continue until something is finished.

items.sentence: Eliot did well in physics, but he only just scraped through his chemistry exam.
items.explanation: 'Scrape through' means to pass/succeed by a narrow margin.

items.sentence: Truth and justice will win through in the end - they always do.
items.explanation: 'Win through' means to succeed after a struggle.

items.sentence: The government's totally committed to carrying through these reforms.
items.explanation: 'Carry through' means to complete/implement successfully.

exercises.label: I
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I want you kids to be ___ your best behaviour tonight.
items.explanation: Collocation: 'on your best behaviour'.

items.sentence: My father's been ___ poor health for a while now.
items.explanation: Collocation: 'in poor health'.

items.sentence: You shouldn't have reacted ___ getting so annoyed.
items.explanation: Collocation: 'react by doing something'.

items.sentence: We were always polite ___ our teachers when I was at school.
items.explanation: Collocation: 'polite to someone'.

items.sentence: I don't think I'll ever be rich, but you live ___ hope, don't you?
items.explanation: Collocation: 'live in hope'.

items.sentence: I hope I live to the grand old age ___ 94!
items.explanation: Collocation: 'live to the age of'.

items.sentence: I am writing ___ response to your letter of 13th July.
items.explanation: Collocation: 'in response to'.

items.sentence: So ___ effect what you're saying is that I'm being made redundant.
items.explanation: Collocation: 'in effect' (basically).

items.sentence: Sue left and, ___ a consequence, I was promoted to Senior Nurse!
items.explanation: Collocation: 'as a consequence'.

items.sentence: I know I did the wrong thing, but I was acting ___ good faith, you know.
items.explanation: Collocation: 'acting in good faith'.

exercises.label: J
exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in the box in the correct form.

items.sentence: Do you know what time the ___ centre opens in the morning?
items.explanation: 'Health centre' is a facility for medical care.

items.sentence: What we do here is ensure that our terminally ill patients get the best ___ of life possible.
items.explanation: 'Quality of life' refers to well-being.

items.sentence: Of course hitting someone's an example of ___ behaviour!
items.explanation: 'Aggressive behaviour' involves physical or verbal attack.

items.sentence: There shouldn't be any ___ effects with these pills, but if there are, let me know.
items.explanation: 'Adverse effects' are harmful side effects.

items.sentence: I hate the thought of standing there making ___ conversation to strangers.
items.explanation: 'Polite conversation' is social small talk.

items.sentence: The consequences could be ___ if you don't start eating properly and get some exercise.
items.explanation: 'Dire consequences' are very serious or terrible.

items.sentence: Some doctors think there's a lot to be said for ___ medicine.
items.explanation: 'Alternative medicine' refers to non-traditional treatments.

exercises.label: K
exercises.instruction: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

items.sentence: I used to be ___ against plastic surgery... / There was ___ silence throughout the room... / ...you'll be in ___ trouble!
items.explanation: Dead against (strongly), dead silence (complete), in dead trouble (serious).

items.sentence: ...they usually respond in ___ . / I didn't say anything of the ___ ! / That was very ___ of you to help...
items.explanation: Respond in kind (similarly), anything of the kind (like that), kind of you (polite/helpful).

items.sentence: The play really came to ___ in the second act. / 'Not on your ___ !' / ...putting your ___ at risk by smoking...
items.explanation: Come to life (become active), Not on your life (certainly not), life at risk (danger).

items.sentence: ...just makes me ___ . / We were worried ___ because... / ...call in ___ this morning.
items.explanation: Makes me sick (disgusts), worried sick (extremely), call in sick (notify absence).

items.sentence: We only went there for a ___ ... / ...have the last ___ , don't you? / It's a ___ a minute when Daniel's around.
items.explanation: For a laugh (fun), have the last laugh (ultimate success), laugh a minute (very funny).

items.sentence: She only said that for ___ ... / ...come into ___ from midnight... / Caffeine has very little ___ on me...
items.explanation: For effect (to impress), come into effect (active), little effect (impact).

exercises.label: L
exercises.instruction: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given.

items.sentence: Spending two weeks in hospital was very different from lying on the beach! (cry) -> Spending two weeks in hospital was ___ lying on the beach!
items.explanation: 'A far cry from' means very different from.

items.sentence: The medicine should start working soon. (benefits) -> You should ___ soon.
items.answer: feel the benefits of the medicine
items.explanation: To 'feel the benefits' of something means it starts working/helping.

items.sentence: I just did what the sergeant ordered me to do, sir. (acting) -> I ___ , sir.
items.answer: was acting on the sergeant's orders
items.explanation: 'Act on orders' means to do what commanded.

items.sentence: I had no idea everyone would react so violently to my suggestion. (cause) -> I had no idea I ___ by suggesting that.
items.answer: would cause such a violent reaction
items.explanation: To 'cause a reaction' links to the violent response.

items.sentence: Don't you think you should be a bit more respectful to them, Edward? (treat) -> Don't you think you should ___ , Edward?
items.answer: treat them with a bit more respect
items.explanation: To 'treat someone with respect' is the collocation.

items.sentence: Alan's got food poisoning. (taken) -> Alan ___ food poisoning.
items.explanation: 'Be taken ill with' is a formal way to say someone has become sick.

exercises.label: M
exercises.instruction: Each of the words in bold is incorrect. Rewrite them correctly.

items.sentence: I think I'll give it a ___ (leave), if you don't mind.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'give something a miss'.

items.sentence: I could have ___ (hit) myself when I invited Claudia...
items.explanation: Idiom: 'kick yourself' (regret).

items.sentence: ...just going to have to ___ (smile) and bear it.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'grin and bear it'.

items.sentence: Hold (keep) your ___ (hair) on!
items.explanation: Idiom: 'Keep your hair on' (stay calm).

items.sentence: ...no need to fly off the ___ (doorknob) like that.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'fly off the handle' (get angry).

items.sentence: ...just to let off ___ (air).
items.explanation: Idiom: 'let off steam'.

items.sentence: ...give them a taste of their own ___ (tablets).
items.explanation: Idiom: 'dose/taste of their own medicine'.

items.sentence: ...everyone round here is up in ___ (guns) about it.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'up in arms' (complaining loudly).

items.sentence: ...never look a gift ___ (cow) in the mouth.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'never look a gift horse in the mouth'.

items.sentence: ...probably better just to let sleeping ___ (cats) lie.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'let sleeping dogs lie'.

items.sentence: ...no point crying over spilt ___ (liquid).
items.explanation: Idiom: 'crying over spilt milk'.

items.sentence: ...hard to keep a ___ (plain) face when...
items.explanation: Idiom: 'keep a straight face'.

exercises.label: N
exercises.instruction: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: Colin became a political ___ when he was at university.
items.explanation: Noun for a person who campaigns for change.

items.sentence: Do you know the ___ of the word 'handsome'?
items.explanation: Noun for the origin of a word.

items.sentence: I know it's a bit annoying but there's no need to ___ to such an extent.
items.explanation: Verb meaning to react too strongly.

items.sentence: Have you got any ___ in your toes at all, Mr Babcock?
items.explanation: Noun meaning physical feeling.

items.sentence: We stood there watching the ___ drama with shock on our faces.
items.explanation: Adjective describing a drama that is developing.

items.sentence: In medicine, ___ is always better than cure.
items.explanation: Noun for the act of stopping something from happening.

items.sentence: If one of our nurses has acted ___ , you can be assured we will take the strongest possible action...
items.explanation: Adverb meaning failing to take proper care.

items.sentence: It's ___ even trying. We'll never get to the hospital in time.
items.explanation: Adjective meaning pointless or unsuccessful.

items.sentence: We were all jumping around ___ in the corridor...
items.explanation: Adverb meaning without patience.

items.sentence: Do you really think your plans are going to come to ___?
items.explanation: Noun meaning the successful result of a plan.

items.sentence: ...there was widespread ___ for the proposed changes...
items.explanation: Noun for the feeling of not agreeing with something.

items.sentence: ...then ___ set in when I reached my early thirties.
items.explanation: Noun for the loss of belief or disappointment.

items.sentence: You're so ___ sometimes!
items.explanation: Adjective describing someone who fails to give enough attention.

items.sentence: She told me she's not ___ in the slightest for what she said.
items.explanation: Adjective meaning feeling sorry.

items.sentence: The whole incident was extremely ___.
items.explanation: Adjective describing a situation that is unfortunate/sad.

items.sentence: There's a lot of ___ with the government at the moment.
items.explanation: Noun for a feeling of being unhappy with a situation.

items.sentence: ...she was completely ___ , so I had no idea at all what she was thinking.
items.explanation: Adjective meaning not reacting or answering.

exercises.label: O
exercises.instruction: Use the word given in capitals at the end of the line to form a word that fits in the space in the same line.

items.sentence: There are many moral issues which remain (1) ___ to this day.
items.explanation: Not yet decided or answered.

items.sentence: All possible treatments known to science are (2) ___.
items.explanation: Not producing the desired effect (usually used for treatments/actions).

items.sentence: The patient's death from this disease... is (3) ___.
items.explanation: Impossible to stop from happening.

items.sentence: They cannot live (4) ___ of machines and 24-hour care.
items.explanation: Without needing help from others.

items.sentence: What if the patient (5) ___ and continually asks...
items.explanation: In a determined way.

items.sentence: But from a moral point of view, we have to (6) ___ that there is a problem.
items.explanation: Verb: to admit the truth of something.

items.sentence: Do the doctors really have the right to be (7) ___ to the patient's wishes?
items.explanation: Not noticing or caring about someone's feelings.

items.sentence: ...opponents of voluntary euthanasia argue that scientific (8) ___ is increasing...
items.explanation: Noun: understanding of a subject.

items.sentence: ...the (9) ___ situation would put doctors in a much worse position.
items.explanation: Happening as a result of something.

items.sentence: Do we... expect doctors to (10) ___ such a difficult and complex issue?
items.explanation: Verb: to deal with or manage.
